title: Salt pillars in Antarctica not icebergs
display_title: Salt pillars in Antarctica not icebergs
wikitext:
[]: Salt pillars in Antarctica, not icebergs             Quote Post by gwern » Sun Aug 07, 2011 1:10 pm This is interesting, and seems never to have been pointed out on EGF before. In NGE TV, when Gendo and Fuyutsuki visit Antarctica in the middle of episode 12, we see skinny static white pillars all around - they are pillars of salt , not ice! This is stated in the Italian NGE Encyclopedia, which has been pretty reliable in the past: http://eva.onegeek.org/pipermail/oldeva/1999-November/032454.html I have this stated in the Italian edition of the Eva encyclopedia, volume 0:6, included in the tape release. In this work, very few things are unconfirmed by Gainax, and what is is made clear to be so, and this is not the case. If you think about it, it's not inconsistent. Second Impact resulted in a giant explosion which vaped the Ice cape and caused a raise on the sea level. Vaporization of the water probably caused dissociation between basic H2O and NaCl, and the latter was condensed in the typical saline formation of 'pillars'. Mind that I'm not saying that if something like Second Impact was to happen things would go exactly that way, only that it is not a big stretch. In any event, Anctartica's climatic situation had gotten such a great change forced in that the last thing I'd expect to be there would be Icebergs.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Aug 07, 2011 1:19 pm From its shape, I doubt they would be normal ice, unless the LCL/water can get colder than freezing point of water. (ALSO assuming that there are barely any wind) I think the only reason they might be ice is because people will have the equation Antarctica=Cold=Ice Also, because Eva likes Biblical references, I would tend to agree this is a rather obvious nudge at the story of Sodom and Gomorrah, and that stupid women who looked back. (Maybe saying that humans are stupid so got turned into salt pillars- which a similar phenomenon can be seen after nuclear explosions) That also reminds me- in this world, is the average temperature of earth greater, or just stratification of the environment depending on the region? (Because Japan had turned into summer for eternity)

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Aug 07, 2011 2:11 pm Hmm, interesting find, Gwern.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 07, 2011 2:18 pm The things at the Antarctic "Dead Sea" are referenced as "pillars of salt" in the script, and also in the Renewal/Platinum booklet. I dropped this tidbit over at ANF , but it's casually shown up here on EGF at least a couple of times as well. I kind of took it for granted, to be honest. BTW, those screenshots suffer from a bit of bloat. Crop them and shrink them, mayhaps?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Aug 07, 2011 2:44 pm So it makes sense for big ol' piles of salt to exist in the Antarctic sea, but why Terminal Dogma?

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Aug 07, 2011 2:51 pm LCL evaporation?

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Sun Aug 07, 2011 3:09 pm Reichu wrote: The things at the Antarctic "Dead Sea" are referenced as "pillars of salt" in the script, and also in the Renewal/Platinum booklet. I dropped this tidbit over at ANF , but it's casually shown up here on EGF at least a couple of times as well. I kind of took it for granted, to be honest.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Aug 08, 2011 12:24 pm NAveryW wrote: So it makes sense for big ol' piles of salt to exist in the Antarctic sea, but why Terminal Dogma?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Aug 08, 2011 12:27 pm Sachi wrote: I can't remember at the moment, but were these pillars in Terminal Domga ever shown in EoE.
